title: Fix for overwriting LD_PRELOAD if it contains libscalene.so and other values
body: It looks like in some cases setup_preload gets called more than once, so it was adding `libscalene.so` to `LD_PRELOAD` the first time then later replacing `LD_PRELOAD`  with just `libscalene.so`, this fixes that case.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -68,11 +68,10 @@ def get_preload_environ(args: argparse.Namespace) -> Dict[str, str]:
                     env['LD_LIBRARY_PATH'] = f'{library_path}:{os.environ["LD_LIBRARY_PATH"]}'
 
                 new_ld_preload = 'libscalene.so'
-                if "LD_PRELOAD" in os.environ and new_ld_preload not in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]:
-                    old_ld_preload = os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]
-                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload + ":" + old_ld_preload
-                else:
+                if "LD_PRELOAD" not in os.environ:
                     env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload
+                elif new_ld_preload not in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"].split(":"):
+                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = f'{new_ld_preload}:{os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]}'
                 # Disable command-line specified PYTHONMALLOC.
                 if "PYTHONMALLOC" in os.environ:
                     # Since the environment dict is updated

steps.operation: Refactor LD_PRELOAD update logic to be idempotent by splitting the existing value on ':' and only prepending libscalene.so when it is not already present as a distinct entry
steps.reason: setup_preload / get_preload_environ can be invoked multiple times; the previous substring-based check could treat an LD_PRELOAD containing libscalene.so plus other values as already satisfied and later overwrite/lose the additional entries. Splitting on ':' ensures membership is checked per-token and preserves other preloads while preventing duplicates.
steps.side_effects: Changes how LD_PRELOAD is constructed for launched processes: it will now reliably preserve any existing preload libraries and avoid duplicate libscalene.so entries. Any downstream logic/tests that asserted the exact LD_PRELOAD string (or relied on substring behavior rather than token matching) may need updating.

step_plan.summary: Fixes LD_PRELOAD handling so libscalene.so is prepended only when not already present as a distinct ':'-separated entry, preserving any other existing preload libraries across repeated setup calls.
